title: Sigrdrífumál
stanzas: When Sigurd rode through the mountains high,
He came upon a warrior bold,
Who lay in sleep on a bed of flame,
And did not stir at his call.
With sword in hand he cut away,
The armor that bound her body tight,
And she awoke with a start and cry,
And gazed upon him in awe and fright.
'I am Sigrdrifa, the Valkyrie great,
I fell asleep for many years,
And none could wake me from my sleep,
But thou hast come and set me free.'
She taught him then the runes of power,
To bring him victory in his fight,
She taught him healing runes so strong,
To cure his wounds and ease his pain.
'If thou wouldst win in battle's rage,
Learn thou the runes that bring thee might,
Carve them upon thy sword of steel,
And victory shall be thine each day.'
'And if thou journeyest upon the sea,
And storms do rage with fury wild,
The runes of safety write thou there,
And thou shalt reach thy port in peace.'
'And if thou lovest a maiden fair,
And wouldst that she return thy love,
Engrave the runes upon thy brow,
And she shall never turn away.'
'But guard thee well against false spells,
And those who would do thee harm,
For magic is a double-edged blade,
That cuts both ways with cruel stroke.'
Thus Sigrdrifa gave him counsel wise,
Before she sent him on his way,
And Sigurd rode with power new,
To win great glory in the fray.
